name: Greenhouse Recruiting API
what_it_does: Enterprise ATS with multiple REST APIs (Harvest for data access, Job Board for public postings, Onboarding for new hire workflows) enabling deep recruiting process automation.
use_cases: Extracting full candidate pipeline and interview feedback data for analytics
Syncing job postings to external boards and career pages via Job Board API
Automating new hire onboarding task assignment via the Onboarding API
Building HR agent workflows that track candidate status and trigger actions on stage changes
not_for: Small companies needing a lightweight ATS — Greenhouse is enterprise-priced
Real-time streaming of candidate events (webhook-based, not streaming)
best_when: Your enterprise organization uses Greenhouse and you need deep, read-heavy integration into candidate data, structured interview feedback, or onboarding workflow automation.
avoid_when: You need a developer-friendly modern ATS with self-serve API access — Greenhouse's API surface is broad but enterprise-gated, and the multiple API families add integration complexity.

auth.notes: Harvest API uses HTTP Basic Auth with API key as username and empty password. Job Board API is public (no auth). Onboarding API uses a separate API key. No OAuth or scoped tokens — each API key has full access to its respective API.

pricing.paid_tiers: Greenhouse is enterprise-priced ATS software; pricing scales with employee count and feature tier (Essential, Advanced, Expert). API access included in subscription. Typical range $6,000–$100,000+/year.
pricing.notes: No public pricing. All plans require a sales engagement and annual contract.

performance.rate_limits: Harvest API: 50 requests per 10 seconds by default. Rate limit details returned in X-RateLimit-* response headers. Bulk retrieval endpoints available to minimize API call count.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Harvest API errors include HTTP status and a JSON body with an errors array containing message strings. No machine-readable error codes — agents must parse error message text to distinguish failure types.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support on any Greenhouse API. Agents must implement duplicate-prevention logic before calling creation or update endpoints.
known_agent_gotchas: Three separate APIs (Harvest, Job Board, Onboarding) with different auth mechanisms require agents to manage multiple credentials and base URLs.
No idempotency — creating candidates or applications twice produces duplicates; external deduplication is required.
Pagination uses Link headers (RFC 5988) rather than a cursor in the response body — less common pattern that many agent frameworks don't handle out of the box.
Webhook payloads are not signed by default on all event types; agents should validate IP allowlisting or payload signatures where available.
Read-heavy API design — many write operations are not available via API and must be done in the Greenhouse UI.

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC 2 Type II certified, GDPR compliant. Basic Auth with API key is functional but dated — no OAuth or token scoping. Separate API keys per API family reduces cross-API blast radius but adds credential management overhead.
